articles.title: 'Your loyalty is for sale': Aaditya slams 'greedy MPs' as Uddhav Sena battles fresh rebellion
articles.description: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Aaditya Thackeray has strongly criticized six Lok Sabha MPs who are reportedly shifting allegiance to the Eknath Shinde faction. Thackeray accused them of betraying voters' mandate and selling their loyalty for political gain, calling their actions a 'shocking example of dirty politics.' The move deepens the internal crisis for Uddhav Thackeray's party, with disciplinary action anticipated against the absent MPs.

articles.title: Watch: JD Vance caught in awkward moment as Qatar PM bypasses him, greets Pak PM instead
articles.description: A recent video from the US-Iran discussions in Switzerland has sparked controversy regarding a perceived diplomatic slight to US Vice President JD Vance. The footage captures Qatar's Prime Minister greeting Pakistan's Prime Minister without acknowledging Vance, igniting conversations online. Despite the video's circulation, none of the governments involved have labeled the exchange as a deliberate snub.

articles.title: School shooting in central Philippines leaves three dead, five injured
articles.description: A shooting at a school in central Philippines on Monday left three individuals dead. Authorities quickly arrested one suspect, who is a minor, while they continue their search for another assailant. The gunfire injured eight people, leaving five hospitalized. The police are diligently investigating the motives behind this tragic event as they attempt to locate the remaining suspect.

articles.title: Why Afghanistan captain Shahidi was punished by ICC after the third ODI vs India
articles.description: Afghanistan captain Hashmatullah Shahidi received an official reprimand and a demerit point from the ICC for damaging the pitch during the third ODI against India. Despite this, Shahidi scored a maiden century, but India secured a dominant nine-wicket victory, completing a 3-0 series sweep. Shahidi acknowledged his team's shortcomings against a strong Indian side.

articles.title: Drone scare over Moscow: Four airports shut, nearly 60 UAVs shot down
articles.description: Moscow's airspace faced disruption as air defence systems intercepted 59 drones targeting the capital, leading to a temporary shutdown of all four airports. This incident highlights ongoing cross-border attacks, with Russia having previously struck Ukraine's Odesa region with a ballistic missile, causing casualties and damage to an agricultural facility. The situation underscores the escalating tensions and the persistent threat of aerial assaults.

articles.title: 'Ain't getting bit by that, bro': Huge uproar after Los Angeles police kill pet dog minutes after family celebrates NBA Knicks win - video
articles.description: Outrage erupted after LAPD released body-camera footage showing officers fatally shooting Jameson, a two-year-old dog, during a disturbance call. Police believed the noise was a woman screaming, but it was a family celebrating the Knicks' NBA championship. The dog, wearing a Knicks jersey, was shot after exiting the apartment. The incident has sparked protests and calls for accountability, with the Mayor ordering a thorough investigation.

articles.title: Oil prices today: Crude falls after US-Iran talks in Switzerland conclude; Brent below $80
articles.description: Following the conclusion of the US-Iran discussions in Switzerland, global oil prices have seen a decrease as Iran has successfully secured waivers for its oil exports. This alleviates concerns regarding possible supply disruptions, leading to a drop in Brent crude futures as well as WTI. The anticipation of increased Iranian oil supply and potential easing of US sanctions, despite regional tensions, has contributed to the market's response.

articles.title: Stock Market Live Updates Today: BSE Sensex opens over 300 points up, crosses 77,000; Nifty50 above 24,100 as US-Iran peace talks progress, oil prices slip marginally
articles.full_text: "Despite the confusing news coming from the West Asia talks, Brent crude is trading below $80. This market signal indicates that further flare up in the conflict is unlikely. However, the situation remains fluid and has to be watched closely. Meanwhile, rupee appreciation and tapering of FPI outflows continues, and this has the potential to impart resilience to the market. The FPI buy figure of Rs 4859 crores on Friday is due to the FTSE rejig and, therefore, is not indicative of any trend. The strengthening of the rupee from the low of 96.96 to the dollar reached on May 20th to 94.32 now is a positive and this trend is likely to continue, given the sharp correction in crude price and the expected big capital flows, particularly from FCNR B deposits. The market momentum is now in the mid and small caps assisted by superior earnings growth potential. Bank Nifty is fundamentally strong and deserves calibrated accumulation,â says VK Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist, Geojit Investments Limited. âOver the weekend, US and Iran made progress during negotiations in Switzerland and agreed upon a plan to reach a final deal within the next 60 days. Oil prices edged lower while the dollar index rose post these developments. The dollar gains follow last weeksâ rally as markets recalibrated Fed expectations away from rate cuts and towards the risk of further hikes. At the time of writing DXY is trading at 100.76. This week, market attention will remain focused on incoming activity and inflation data, alongside central bank commentary. In India, markets will track May infrastructure output and June PMI prints for cues on growth momentum. Globally, flash PMI readings across the Euro Area and the U.S. will provide cues on the strength of manufacturing and services activity, while the U.S. PCE and core PCE inflation prints will be key for assessing the Fedâs policy path. Commentary from ECB President Lagarde and Fed officials will also be watched for guidance on the evolving policy outlook,â says HDFC Bankâs Treasury Research Desk. Asian equities moved higher on Monday after Iranian negotiators indicated that discussions with the United States had made headway, easing concerns that the fragile peace process was at risk of collapsing. Supporting the positive sentiment, officials from Qatar and Pakistan issued a joint statement saying that the opening round of negotiations had concluded successfully and that progress had been achieved toward establishing a framework for a comprehensive agreement within the next 60 days. The developments followed earlier tensions after US President Donald Trump warned of possible renewed military action against Iran, even as Vice President JD Vance held the first direct talks with Iranian representatives under the interim peace arrangement. The negotiations were also clouded by Tehran's announcement that it had once again shut the Strait of Hormuz. Shipping data showed a decline in vessel traffic through the key waterway, with 32 ships passing through on Friday compared with 26 on Saturday. In the commodity market, Brent crude futures erased their initial gains and slipped 0.4% to $80.17 per barrel, significantly below the peak of $126.41 reached in May. US crude, however, remained 1.2% higher at $77.52 a barrel. Japan's Nikkei surged 1.9%, extending last week's nearly 8% rally that carried the index to fresh record highs. South Korea's benchmark market advanced another 2.6% after soaring more than 11% in the previous week, driven by strong demand for semiconductor-related stocks. MSCI's broad Asia-Pacific index excluding Japan rose 1.0%, while China's blue-chip index remained largely unchanged. US equity futures recovered from steeper early declines, with S&P 500 futures down 0.2% and Nasdaq futures lower by 0.3%. In Europe, EURO STOXX 50 futures slipped 0.1%, DAX futures were broadly flat, and FTSE futures edged up 0.1%. âGoing ahead, considering the current chart structure, the index is likely to extend its upward move and test the levels of 24300 followed by 24450 in the near term. On the downside, the zone of 24060â24030 is expected to act as immediate support for the index,â says Sudeep Shah, Head - Technical and Derivatives Research at SBI Securities. The banking benchmark index, Bank Nifty, has been outperforming the frontline indices over the past couple of weeks. Continuing this trend, the index witnessed a consolidation breakout on Thursday and formed a sizeable bullish candle on the daily chart, indicating strong buying interest and sustained momentum. From a technical perspective, the index is currently trading comfortably above its short-term as well as long-term moving averages, reinforcing the prevailing bullish undertone. Additionally, the daily RSI is positioned above the 60 mark and continues to trend higher, which further supports the positive momentum in the index. Going ahead, the index is likely to continue its upward trajectory and test the levels of 58500 followed by 59100 in the short term. On the downside, the zone of 57500â57400 is expected to act as immediate support, providing a cushion against any interim pullbacks, says Sudeep Shah. The movement of the Indian rupee this week is expected to be influenced largely by the sustainability of lower crude oil prices and the direction of the US dollar, while the domestic bond market is likely to remain sensitive to the pace of foreign investor inflows. The rupee gained 0.8% last week to close at 94.32 against the US dollar, registering its strongest weekly performance in 11 weeks. The rally was primarily supported by a decline in crude oil prices following the peace agreement between the US and Iran. During the week, the currency briefly strengthened to 94.18, its highest level since May 7, before giving back part of its gains as the dollar advanced on rising expectations that the US Federal Reserve could increase interest rates later this year. âThere is distinct change in the FPI action since June 15th. During the week ending 19th June FPIs bought stocks on three days and sold only on two days. For the week ending June 19th the FPIs have net bought equity for Rs 3386 crores in the cash market. It can be concluded that the relentless selling of FPIs in India is over. The principal reason for this change in FPI activity is the stability and slow appreciation in the rupee. Rupee has made a decent recovery from the low of 96.96 to the dollar touched on May 20th to 94.34, the closing rate on 19th June. Substantial inflows of dollars are expected via the FCNR B bonds in FY27. This, along with crash in Brent crude to $80 levels will help India in financing the Current Account Deficit in FY27 without any stress. Rupee is likely to appreciate further in the coming weeks. In the context of appreciating rupee, FPIs are unlikely to sell significantly, going forward. The concentration risk in investing in a few stocks in South Korea and Taiwan is making FPIs a bit worried. However, the continuing AI trade and the huge expected profitability of companies like Samsung, SK Hynix and TSMC make these stocks still attractive. Therefore, these stocks may still be bought on declines and FPIs may sell in India on rallies. The better-than-expected FY26 earnings growth of 15.6% for Nifty 500 is providing fundamental support and resilience to the market. However, these stocks may poor monsoon, so far this year , is becoming a concern,â says V K Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist, Geojit Investments Limited.

articles.title: Gold, Silver Rate Today Live Updates: International gold, silver prices rebound on US-Iran peace talks
articles.full_text: Gold and silver prices began the week on a positive note on the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), recovering after two consecutive sessions of decline as easing crude oil prices followed indications of progress in the ongoing US-Iran peace negotiations. However, the outlook for precious metals continued to face pressure from expectations of higher US interest rates after the Federal Reserve's recent hawkish commentary. In the domestic derivatives market, MCX gold futures for August 2026 delivery advanced Rs 784 to Rs 1,47,987 per 10 grams. Silver futures for July 2026 delivery, however, were trading lower by Rs 3,921, or 1.6%, at Rs 2,37,106 per kilogram. The previous session had witnessed heavy selling, with silver falling more than 2% and gold declining 1.40%. Crude oil prices remain an important factor for bullion markets. Elevated oil prices tend to increase inflationary pressures and reinforce expectations of tighter monetary policy. In such an environment, gold often loses some of its appeal because, unlike interest-bearing assets, it does not generate any yield. According to Jateen Trivedi, VP Research Analyst - Commodity and Currency at LKP Securities, gold remained under pressure throughout last week as lower energy prices, appreciation in the rupee and the US Federal Reserve's hawkish policy stance combined to weigh on sentiment. He added that a stronger rupee reduces the effective cost of imported gold, which in turn exerts additional downward pressure on domestic bullion prices. Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh's strong focus on inflation during last week's press conference, without offering additional guidance on what conditions could trigger a rate increase, led investors to increasingly expect a future rate hike and pushed bond yields higher. Many major global brokerage firms now expect the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged through the remainder of 2026. This marks a shift from earlier expectations at the beginning of the year, when markets had anticipated two rate cuts, as policymakers continue to contend with elevated inflation risks and a resilient labour market. A spokesperson for Iran's foreign ministry said the four-party talks being held in Switzerland had made encouraging progress, according to Iran's Press TV. Negotiations between Iran and the United States entered a second day in Switzerland on Monday following a tense start that featured Tehran's announcement of another closure of the Strait of Hormuz and renewed warnings from US President Donald Trump about the possibility of resuming military action against Iran. Brent crude futures declined 0.5% after Iran signalled progress in the discussions, helping ease fears of persistent inflation and prolonged high interest rates globally. Physical demand for gold in India remained subdued last week despite prices falling to their lowest levels in about two-and-a-half months, as volatility persisted. In China, the world's largest consumer of the precious metal, gold traded at a discount. Data released by Swiss customs authorities showed that Switzerland's gold exports declined 9% in May compared with the previous month, as lower shipments to India and Hong Kong outweighed stronger exports to Britain and China. Gold prices climbed more than 1% on Monday, recovering from a decline that had pushed the metal to its lowest level in over a week during the previous session. The rebound came as Brent crude oil prices eased after Iran reported progress in ongoing peace negotiations with the United States, reducing concerns about inflationary pressures and the prospect of higher interest rates. Spot gold advanced 1.2% to $4,209.03 per ounce as of 0112 GMT. Meanwhile, US gold futures for August delivery slipped 0.5% to $4,225.80.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.description: The National Testing Agency has published the final answer key for CUET UG 2026 after reviewing objections raised by candidates. Seven questions have been dropped from the examination. Conducted between May 11 and June 7, the test saw over 15.6 lakh registrations. With the final key now available, universities are expected to begin their admission processes based on CUET scores.

articles.description: Bijendra Nath Tiwari, brother of actor Pankaj Tripathi, has been hospitalized in Patna with critical injuries after an attack in Bihar. The incident, reportedly stemming from an old dispute in Belsand village, left him severely wounded. Doctors have described his condition as extremely critical, necessitating advanced medical care. Police have formed a special team to investigate and apprehend the attackers.

articles.title: Sona says Bollywood heartbreak songs are 'reserved for men'
articles.description: Singer Sona Mohapatra has brought to light the stark gender disparity in Bollywood music, where male artists overwhelmingly dominate the genres of romance and heartbreak, limiting opportunities for female vocalists. She emphasizes that female singers are frequently confined to the background chorus in duets, challenging the male-oriented framework of the industry. Mohapatra asserts that this entrenched issue prevents the rise of iconic women in the music scene.

We don't just report; we tell tales of stardom and stories untold. Whether it's the rise of a new sensation or the seasoned journey of an industry veteran, the TOI Entertainment Desk is your front-row seat to the fascinating narratives that shape the entertainment landscape.
